meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve Stalled SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement high-converting user onboarding to overcome stagnant SaaS development. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Most freelancers stall because they build features, not experiences. By implementing a data-driven user onboarding flow using Antigravity for UI scaffolding and Claude Code for logic, you transform basic CRUD apps into sticky, professional-grade SaaS products. This approach reduces churn, clarifies value, and significantly accelerates your project delivery timelines.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding out features, but your users drop off within minutes of signing up. You’re not just dealing with a bug; you’re dealing with a 'useless app' perception. It is the silent killer of freelance careers, turning high-effort projects into abandoned GitHub repositories.

But the gap between a basic app and a premium SaaS isn't more code—it's the first sixty seconds. By automating your onboarding flow, you stop being a developer who writes functions and start being an architect who builds retention. Let’s ship a high-converting experience that keeps your clients happy and your projects profitable.

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Identify the 'Aha!' moment of your app and map the shortest path to it. We need to strip away friction points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a concise 3-step user onboarding flow UI structure for a SaaS dashboard that guides users to their first core value action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create onboarding-flow component with state management for a 3-step progress indicator'

steps.title: Build the Skeleton UI
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate clean, accessible components that don't distract from the onboarding task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive overlay modal component for onboarding, including a progress bar and a skip button, styled for a professional SaaS aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add tailwind css styling to the newly created onboarding modal components'

steps.title: Implement State Tracking
steps.description: Ensure the app remembers where the user left off so they don't have to restart the onboarding process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React hook to track onboarding completion status in local storage and conditionally render the onboarding modal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor app.tsx to check onboarding status from local storage before rendering the main dashboard'

steps.title: Integrate Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Use subtle visual cues to highlight core features, reducing the cognitive load on new users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable tooltip component that attaches to specific DOM elements to explain feature utility during the onboarding sequence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement tooltip logic to trigger on mount for the primary call-to-action button'

steps.title: Final QA and Edge Case Handling
steps.description: Use Claude Code to stress test the onboarding flow for mobile responsiveness and error states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the onboarding flow, checking for completion status persistence and escape key closing functionality.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run test suite and fix any identified edge cases for mobile screen sizes'

pro_tips: Never make onboarding mandatory; always offer a clear 'Skip' option to respect power users.
Show progress bars; psychological completion bias keeps users engaged longer.
Use 'Empty States' as onboarding opportunities, not just placeholders for missing data.
Trigger onboarding based on user behavior (e.g., hovering for 3s), not just on page load.
Keep copy minimal; if the UI needs a paragraph of explanation, the UI itself is the problem.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, run a quick usability test with a peer, and iterate based on where they click first.
content.conversion_block: Stop building apps that get ignored and start delivering experiences that drive retention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
